meta.title: Documentation - LoL Neural Network
meta.description: Complete technical documentation for the competitive League of Legends match prediction model.

toc.title: Table of Contents
toc.description: Complete technical documentation for the competitive League of Legends match prediction system.

hero.title: Neural Network for Competitive League of Legends Match Prediction
hero.description: End-to-end machine learning system built with PyTorch to predict professional League of Legends match outcomes using champion embeddings, historical stats, matchups, synergies, and a hybrid architecture with dynamic gating.

overview.p1: This project is a machine learning system designed to predict outcomes of professional competitive League of Legends matches using historical esports data from 2023 to 2026.
overview.p2: The model's main goal is to learn complex patterns related to competitive drafts, champion matchups, historical player and team performance, pick synergies, and recent metagame trends.
overview.p3: The system was built with PyTorch and a dual hybrid architecture that combines trainable champion embeddings with a numeric branch of advanced historical statistics.

objective.p1: This project's goal was not only to train a model with good accuracy, but to build a complete competitive prediction system capable of:
items: Deploying inference in production via API.
Consuming the model from a modern web interface.

dataset.p1: The dataset comes from professional matches across multiple international competitive leagues.

pipeline.p1: The processing pipeline was designed to simulate a real temporal prediction environment.
pipeline.flow: Raw Matches → Cleaning → Feature Engineering → Scaling → Encoding → Tensors → Neural Network
pipeline.p2: All data was sorted chronologically before generating historical statistics to avoid future contamination.

leakage.p1: One of the most important issues in sports models is temporal data leakage.
leakage.p2: To prevent it, all historical statistics were computed using only information prior to each match.

features.p1: Feature engineering is one of the most important components of the project.

architecture.p1: The model uses a dual hybrid architecture composed of:

embeddings.p1: Each champion is represented by a trainable embedding of dimension 16.
embeddings.p2: Embeddings let the model learn semantic relationships between similar champions and capture complex competitive metagame patterns.

roles.p1: For each competitive role, the model generates individual representations using the difference between opposing embeddings.
roles.p2: This models relative advantages between specific picks within each competitive lane.

interactions.p1: El modelo calcula interacciones pairwise entre roles para capturar sinergias complejas entre composiciones.

numeric.p1: The numeric branch processes scaled historical statistics using StandardScaler.
numeric.p2: This branch is especially important for handling new champions or rare picks that rarely appear during training.

gate.p1: The gate was designed to address new champions and sparse data.
gate.p2: The mechanism dynamically learns how much to trust embeddings versus numeric statistics depending on match context.

training.p1: The model was trained using BCEWithLogitsLoss and the Adam optimizer.

regularization.p1: Multiple regularization techniques were applied to improve generalization and reduce overfitting.

results.p1: The model significantly outperforms all traditional winrate-only baselines.

deployment.p1: The model was exported and deployed via an API for real-time inference.

frontend.p1: The project also includes a modern web interface to consume the model API.

conclusion.p1: This project implements a complete end-to-end machine learning system for competitive League of Legends match prediction.
conclusion.p2: The combination of advanced feature engineering, embeddings, role interactions, and dynamic gating captures complex competitive metagame patterns and far exceeds traditional baseline models.
conclusion.p3: Beyond the model itself, the project also covers deployment, APIs, and a web frontend, making it a production-oriented complete ML system.
